title: elaeocarpus ramiflorus
intro: Elaeocarpus ramiflorus, also known as the Mountain Apple or Blueberry Ash, is a plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. Elaeocarpus ramiflorus is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies as a poultice or salve.
